sections.context: ADR-0013 makes Creator Onboarding Interview → ProfileGen the reference vertical slice: the one flow built end-to-end so every pattern (auth, db, the @resonance/ai agent+tool registry, streaming UI, Server Actions + Zod, pgvector matching, the full test pyramid) is proven once and future flows are built by replication. It is also the first real backlog for the firstmate-only agentic workflow — decomposed along package boundaries so each step is a self-contained crewmate task in its own worktree.
sections.approach: Build the slice package-by-package, because in this monorepo package boundaries are the parallelization boundaries (ADR-0003) — one step maps to one firstmate crewmate in one treehouse worktree. Foundations first: db schema/migration and core Zod schemas + ports, which everything else consumes. Then ai (Weave interview + ProfileGen agents via the registry, ADR-0009) and ui (streaming Weave rail + Figma profile panels) proceed in parallel against those shared types. Then apps/web wires them with Zod-validated Server Actions behind an authenticated creator session (auth magic-link already landed). Finally an E2E pass proves the flow and seeds the pgvector matching backbone by embedding the generated profile (ADR-0010). Each step ships with its own tests per ADR-0011.

steps.title: db: schema + migration for users, creator_profiles, and profile embeddings
core: Zod schemas, domain types, and ports for interview messages + creator profile
ai: Weave interview agent + ProfileGen agent with saveProfile tool, plus profile embedding
ui: streaming Weave interview rail + generated-profile panels from Figma tokens
web: Zod-validated Server Actions + interview route wiring ui ↔ ai ↔ db behind creator auth
e2e + matching: Playwright flow test end-to-end and pgvector embedding/matching verification

risks: Streaming agent output wired through RSC / Server Actions is fiddly — mismatched streaming contracts between ai and ui can surface only at the web wiring step.
pgvector migration and embedding dimensionality must match the model in @resonance/ai; a dim mismatch fails silently at query time (ADR-0010).
The saveProfile agent tool crosses the ai→db boundary; transaction/ownership boundaries must be explicit so a partial generation cannot persist a half-written profile.
Figma-derived UI can drift from design tokens if built without the Figma MCP context — authorize Figma before the ui step starts.
E2E of a streaming interview is prone to flakiness; the test must assert on settled state, not intermediate tokens.
acceptance: A creator can complete the Weave interview and receive a generated profile persisted to creator_profiles.
The generated profile is embedded into pgvector, proving the matching backbone end-to-end (ADR-0010).
The interview route is gated behind an authenticated creator session.
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test pass across affected packages, and each package step ships with its own tests (ADR-0011).
A Playwright E2E drives the full flow from front door to generated profile.

sections.context: The deep-module design tenet now lives in .claude/skills/module-design/SKILL.md, but its mapping to Resonance is still marked 'provisional — to be ratified in ADR-0017.' The tenet is not ratified, not indexed for priming, and not enforced at any workflow juncture. So agents designing a package interface, a port/adapter, a query helper, or a component's props have no consistent vocabulary and no explicit depth criterion, and reviews cannot flag a shallow module or a leaky seam. This work formalizes the tenet and weaves it into every point where design happens or design quality is enforced. It is NOT a pivot: it names and makes explicit what Resonance already does implicitly — a @resonance/* package is a module, its src/index.ts entrypoint is a seam, a port is an interface at a seam, and the PGlite/stub fake is the second adapter. The full juncture map and kickoff brief are in docs/module-design-integration-handoff.md; this decomposes the entry-point seed resonance-d279.
sections.approach: Follow the handoff's suggested order as a dependency DAG so every cross-reference points at an already-landed source of truth. ADR-0017 is authored first as the single source of truth and is indexed into mulch the moment it lands; everything else links to it. docs/conventions.md then becomes the operational home — the rules agents actually consult. Those two foundations, in series, unblock a genuinely parallel wave: the root CLAUDE.md pointer line, the four recipe reframings, the planning + prompt-template updates, and the review dimension — each an independent surface depending only on the ADR + conventions. A final verify seed confirms pnpm check:workspace passes, the ADR is indexed, and ml prime surfaces the tenet where an agent would design. Two design tensions from the handoff are resolved INSIDE the ADR (step 1) and treated as acceptance conditions, not afterthoughts: (1) keep 'boundary' for ADR-0003's package/context/ownership sense and introduce 'seam' for the finer-grained location of an interface — a package boundary IS one kind of seam; do NOT rename ADR-0003, add the mapping in the new ADR; (2) justify Resonance's model-ahead ports (StoragePort deferred, PaymentsPort stubbed) as satisfying the two-adapter rule — the test fake is the second adapter and the variance is known/imminent, so 'known future variance + a test fake' counts, while a truly single-adapter, no-known-variance seam is the one to avoid. Chosen over one monolithic edit because the DAG makes the middle wave parallelizable in Phase 2 and prevents dangling forward-references.

alternatives.name: One monolithic doc edit touching every juncture in a single seed
alternatives.rejected_because: Not parallelizable, and its cross-references would point at not-yet-written targets (the ADR, the conventions section).

alternatives.name: A standalone new recipe (design-a-seam / add-a-port)
alternatives.rejected_because: Deferred: folded into the conventions 'Module design' section to keep the surface small (the handoff marks this recipe optional). Promote to its own recipe later only if a seam-introduction task recurs.

alternatives.name: Split the four recipe reframings into four separate seeds
alternatives.rejected_because: Rejected for now: each is a one-paragraph edit within one surface (.claude/skills), so four PRs would be overkill orchestration; kept as one seed touching four files. Easy to split if preferred.

alternatives.name: Fold the review dimension into conventions.md rather than its own seed
alternatives.rejected_because: Rejected: review enforcement is a distinct juncture and deserves an explicit, referenceable checklist so no-mistakes / code-review can point at it.

steps.title: adr: write ADR-0017 'Design deep modules' (vocabulary + principles + the boundary/seam and model-ahead-ports resolutions); add its README row; add the mulch architecture reference record; update SKILL.md's 'provisional' note to point at the ratified ADR

conventions: add a 'Module design' section to docs/conventions.md — glossary, the three interface questions, the deletion test, interface-as-test-surface, the one/two-adapter rule, the three testability rules, and how to record design learnings with the vocabulary (the operational home)
claude-md: add one warm-layer golden-rule pointer line to root CLAUDE.md — 'Design deep modules — small interface, lots hidden; the package entrypoint is the seam (ADR-0017, conventions.md)'

recipes: reframe the four recipes (scaffold-domain-package, add-ai-agent, add-db-migration, add-ui-component-from-figma) as deep modules — one paragraph each, using the exact vocabulary and pointing to conventions.md

planning+prompts: add deep-module criteria to /feature Phase 1 + the sd plan authoring guidance (name each step's interface/seam; sanity-check depth via the deletion test; approach identifies seams), and to the subagent + firstmate crewmate prompt templates

review: add 'module depth' as a review dimension — a repo-owned checklist of the shallow-module / leaky-interface / test-past-the-interface / deps-created-not-accepted / side-effects-not-results smells, referenced from the no-mistakes + /code-review guidance

verify: confirm pnpm check:workspace passes, ADR-0017 is indexed (ml prime architecture surfaces it), and ml prime surfaces the tenet where an agent designs — spot-check one design domain; fix any term drift across the junctures

risks: Vocabulary drift: 'boundary' vs 'seam' gets muddled if the ADR-0003 mapping isn't stated crisply. Mitigate by making the resolution explicit in ADR-0017 and NOT renaming ADR-0003 — a package boundary is one kind of seam.

One-fact-one-home violation (ADR-0016): the same operative rule restated in two hot stores. The ADR holds the why, conventions holds the operative rule, CLAUDE.md/recipes/prompts link — they must not duplicate the rule text.
Over-scoping into new architecture. This is formalization + vocabulary unification only; no package, port, or code changes. Guard the scope.

The review dimension has no repo-local home today (no-mistakes and /code-review are external plugin skills). It must land as a repo-owned checklist that review guidance references, or it won't actually be enforced.

SKILL.md currently says the Resonance mapping is 'provisional — to be ratified in ADR-0017.' If step 1 doesn't update that note, a dangling 'provisional' remains after the ADR lands (folded into step 1).
acceptance: ADR-0017 'Design deep modules' exists and is Accepted, has a row in docs/adr/README.md, and is indexed by a mulch architecture `reference` record so `ml prime architecture` surfaces it without loading the prose.

ADR-0017 explicitly records BOTH resolutions: (a) 'boundary' keeps ADR-0003's package/context sense and 'seam' is introduced for the finer interface location, with ADR-0003 NOT renamed; (b) model-ahead ports (StoragePort, PaymentsPort) satisfy the two-adapter rule because the test fake is the second adapter and the variance is known/imminent.

docs/conventions.md has a 'Module design' section carrying the glossary, the three interface questions, the deletion test, interface-as-test-surface, the one/two-adapter rule, and the three testability rules — the operational home other junctures link to.
The module-design SKILL.md's 'provisional — to be ratified in ADR-0017' note is updated to reference the now-ratified ADR.
Root CLAUDE.md's golden rules include a one-line deep-module pointer to ADR-0017 + conventions.md.
Each of the four recipes frames its unit (package / agent registry / query helper / component props) as a module with a small interface using the exact vocabulary, and points to conventions.md.

/feature Phase 1 + the sd-plan authoring guidance and the subagent/crewmate prompt templates instruct agents to decompose into deep modules, name each step's interface/seam, and sanity-check depth via the deletion test.

A 'module depth' review dimension (checklist) exists and is referenced from the review guidance, enumerating the shallow-module / leaky-interface / test-past-the-interface / deps-created-not-accepted / side-effects-not-results smells.
pnpm check:workspace passes and the tenet uses one consistent vocabulary across the ADR, conventions, CLAUDE.md, recipes, prompts, and review (no term drift).

sections.context: The reference slice was built mock-first: a runtime RESONANCE_FAKES flag swaps a fake for the live adapter in resolveModel / resolveEmbedder / resolveMail. This kept CI and the full-flow E2E green with zero credentials, but it MASKED live gaps — the Resend transport and the direct-Anthropic/Voyage provider paths were never implemented, yet everything was green, because no test ever exercised a live path. The env-flag fork also lives in shipped runtime code (hard to read) and drifted into a fake-of-a-fake (apps/web/lib/fake-profile-model.ts) because the text-only fake model can't satisfy profile-gen's tool call. ADR-0018 reverses the approach: live-by-default runtime code, fakes injected only in tests, and a credential-gated live-smoke gate so a green build can't hide broken live wiring.
sections.approach: DI-ize the three swap seams (model, embeddings, mail) so shipped code is live-only and every fake is a test-injected dependency, and implement the missing live provider fallbacks so 'live-by-default' actually functions with whatever key is present. The one place a runtime seam was genuinely needed — the full-flow E2E of the running app (you can't DI into a separate server process) — is re-pointed at a credential-gated live-smoke gate rather than a runtime fake. Work package-by-package (ADR-0003): ai + auth seams in parallel, then web removes its fake injection, then the E2E/live-smoke decision, then docs + diagram sync. Fakes move to test-only modules (ai/test, @resonance/db/testing, createFakeMail) that unit/component tests keep using — the fast inner loop stays free and deterministic.

steps.title: ai: live-by-default model + embedding providers (Gateway OR direct Anthropic/Voyage by key), remove the RESONANCE_FAKES branch; move fakes to ai/test for injection
auth: DI-ize resolveMail — live Resend when keyed else fail-closed stub, no fakes branch; createFakeMail stays a test-injected double
web: delete the fake-of-a-fake (fake-profile-model.ts) + the RESONANCE_FAKES injection in generateDraft; rely on the live-by-default ai seam

e2e: DECISION — re-point the full-flow Playwright test to a credential-gated live-smoke gate (verify:live: one real model call + embedding + email + DB write); remove the E2E-only fake seams (peekLoginCode, /api/test/last-otp) or isolate them as an explicit E2E-only harness
docs + diagram: sync ai/auth/web CLAUDE.md to no-runtime-fakes + DI + live-smoke; update the architecture diagram (ADR-0015) for the activated live services; confirm ADR-0018 is indexed

risks: The full-flow E2E becomes credential-gated + non-deterministic once live — assert on settled shape/state, not LLM content, and keep it out of the fast inner loop.
Live-smoke needs secrets available to CI (or nightly/pre-deploy) plus a small token/email budget.
Removing peekLoginCode + /api/test/last-otp breaks the current deterministic OTP E2E — re-point it in the same step (step 4).
resolveModel/resolveEmbedder provider selection must FAIL CLOSED with a clear error when no key is present — never silently no-op or fake.
acceptance: No RESONANCE_FAKES (or equivalent) branch remains in shipped runtime code — grep clean across packages/*/src + apps/web, excluding *.test.* and test-only modules.
Fakes exist only in test-only modules and are supplied via DI; no fake-of-a-fake remains.
Live-by-default works with EITHER a Vercel AI Gateway key OR direct ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + VOYAGE_API_KEY.

A credential-gated live-smoke gate exercises model + embedding + email + DB writes for real and is wired to run pre-release; the fast inner loop (typecheck/lint/test) still needs no credentials and is green.
ai/auth/web CLAUDE.md and the architecture diagram reflect the new approach; ADR-0018 is indexed in docs/adr/README.md.
